verses.text: 'There is therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Jesus'; the Spirit hath made me free. No condemnation. In Christ, the sentence is lifted.
'There is therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Jesus'; the Spirit hath made me free. No condemnation. In Christ, the sentence is lifted.
What the law could not do, God did, 'sending his own Son,' condemning sin in the flesh. The law fulfilled. Christ did for us what the law never could.
What the law could not do, God did, 'sending his own Son,' condemning sin in the flesh. The law fulfilled. Christ did for us what the law never could.
They after the flesh mind the flesh; 'the carnal mind is enmity against God.' Two minds. The fleshly heart cannot please God.
They after the flesh mind the flesh; 'the carnal mind is enmity against God.' Two minds. The fleshly heart cannot please God.
They after the flesh mind the flesh; 'the carnal mind is enmity against God.' Two minds. The fleshly heart cannot please God.
They after the flesh mind the flesh; 'the carnal mind is enmity against God.' Two minds. The fleshly heart cannot please God.
Ye are in the Spirit, if His Spirit dwell in you; He shall 'quicken your mortal bodies.' Indwelt. The Spirit is the mark and life of the believer.
Ye are in the Spirit, if His Spirit dwell in you; He shall 'quicken your mortal bodies.' Indwelt. The Spirit is the mark and life of the believer.
Ye are in the Spirit, if His Spirit dwell in you; He shall 'quicken your mortal bodies.' Indwelt. The Spirit is the mark and life of the believer.
'As many as are led by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God.' Led of the Spirit. The Spirit's leading proves our sonship.
'As many as are led by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God.' Led of the Spirit. The Spirit's leading proves our sonship.
'As many as are led by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God.' Led of the Spirit. The Spirit's leading proves our sonship.
Ye received 'the Spirit of adoption, whereby we cry, Abba, Father'; heirs of God, joint-heirs with Christ. Adopted. We are made children and heirs.
Ye received 'the Spirit of adoption, whereby we cry, Abba, Father'; heirs of God, joint-heirs with Christ. Adopted. We are made children and heirs.
Ye received 'the Spirit of adoption, whereby we cry, Abba, Father'; heirs of God, joint-heirs with Christ. Adopted. We are made children and heirs.
'The sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory which shall be revealed.' Light affliction. Present pain is nothing to coming glory.
'The sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory which shall be revealed.' Light affliction. Present pain is nothing to coming glory.
'The sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory which shall be revealed.' Light affliction. Present pain is nothing to coming glory.
'The sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory which shall be revealed.' Light affliction. Present pain is nothing to coming glory.
Creation groans; we wait for 'the redemption of our body'; we are 'saved by hope.' Patient hope. We await with creation the full redemption.
Creation groans; we wait for 'the redemption of our body'; we are 'saved by hope.' Patient hope. We await with creation the full redemption.
Creation groans; we wait for 'the redemption of our body'; we are 'saved by hope.' Patient hope. We await with creation the full redemption.
Creation groans; we wait for 'the redemption of our body'; we are 'saved by hope.' Patient hope. We await with creation the full redemption.
The Spirit helps our infirmities, 'maketh intercession for us with groanings which cannot be uttered.' Helped in prayer. When we know not what to ask, the Spirit pleads.
The Spirit helps our infirmities, 'maketh intercession for us with groanings which cannot be uttered.' Helped in prayer. When we know not what to ask, the Spirit pleads.
'All things work together for good to them that love God'; whom He foreknew, called, justified, and glorified. Sure chain. God's purpose carries His own through to glory.
'All things work together for good to them that love God'; whom He foreknew, called, justified, and glorified. Sure chain. God's purpose carries His own through to glory.
'All things work together for good to them that love God'; whom He foreknew, called, justified, and glorified. Sure chain. God's purpose carries His own through to glory.
'If God be for us, who can be against us?' He that gave His Son will 'freely give us all things.' Unfailing gift. The God who gave His Son withholds no good.
'If God be for us, who can be against us?' He that gave His Son will 'freely give us all things.' Unfailing gift. The God who gave His Son withholds no good.
Who shall charge God's elect? 'It is Christ that died... who also maketh intercession for us.' No accuser. The Judge has justified; the Advocate pleads.
Who shall charge God's elect? 'It is Christ that died... who also maketh intercession for us.' No accuser. The Judge has justified; the Advocate pleads.
Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? 'in all these things we are more than conquerors.' Triumphant. Trouble cannot sunder us from His love.
Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? 'in all these things we are more than conquerors.' Triumphant. Trouble cannot sunder us from His love.
Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? 'in all these things we are more than conquerors.' Triumphant. Trouble cannot sunder us from His love.
'Neither death, nor life... shall be able to separate us from the love of God, which is in Christ Jesus.' Unbreakable bond. Nothing in all creation can part us from God's love.
'Neither death, nor life... shall be able to separate us from the love of God, which is in Christ Jesus.' Unbreakable bond. Nothing in all creation can part us from God's love.
